title: Chamaecrista major
intro: Chamaecrista major, also known as partridge pea, is a perennial legume native to the Americas, characterized by its spreading habit and clusters of small, yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Chamaecrista major include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Chamaecrista major include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and immune function.
taxonomy: Chamaecrista major is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is a legume native to tropical Africa. Chamaecrista major is commonly known as the "sensitive plant" due to its compound leaves that fold up when touched. The plant is classified within the genus Chamaecrista, which consists of about 250 species of tropical and subtropical plants. Chamaecrista major is further classified as a member of the subfamily Caesalpinioideae.
